question: Have the brown corrugated cardboard boxes on the conveyor belt been changed to a semi-transparent frosted acrylic material?
Are faint silhouettes of items visible inside the modified boxes, consistent with the semi-transparent instruction?
Is the original brown corrugated cardboard texture completely replaced by a smooth, frosted appearance (white, grey, or clear)?
Do the modified boxes exhibit realistic specular highlights and light scattering consistent with acrylic material under overhead lighting?
Is the material change applied consistently to all cardboard boxes on the conveyor belt, including those in the background and on different levels?
Do the white poly mailer bags and other non-cardboard packages on the conveyor belt remain opaque and unchanged in material?
Are the cardboard scraps and debris on the floor preserved as original opaque cardboard (not changed to acrylic)?
Are the workers' safety vests, clothing, and physical appearance preserved without color shifts or distortion?
Is the metal structure of the conveyor belt system (rollers, frames) preserved without taking on the acrylic texture?
Are background elements such as the 'BLACK FRIDAY RUSH' banner, ceiling lights, and floor markings unchanged?
Do the modified boxes retain their original rectangular shapes and rigid edges without looking melted or crumpled?
Is the image free of significant artifacts such as floating silhouettes, jagged edges, or global blurring?
